payload.text:
**Defining the Core Structure**

I'm now zeroing in on the essential architecture of this School AI Tool Hub. My immediate focus is fleshing out the high-level sections: Home, Dashboard, Administration, Academic Affairs (with its sub-sections), and Student Affairs. I'm prioritizing the core layout and relationships between these modules, ensuring a logical flow for the user.


**Expanding the Feature Set**

I'm deep into the feature set now, breaking down tools by department and function. This includes language, STEM, guidance, and admin tools, along with batch data handling. I'm visualizing an Excel-like interface to manage bulk operations. I've also finalized the core tech stack: React 18+, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Lucide React, Framer Motion, and the Gemini API through `@google/genai`, leveraging `recharts` for dashboard analytics, and `papaparse` for CSV data importing. I will use a standard file input, or create a simulated batch interface instead of react-dropzone. Metadata is saved in a JSON file.


**Mapping the Front End Components**

I've been mapping out the front-end components and file structure in more detail. This includes `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, and the various `components` like `Sidebar`, `Header`, `ToolCard`, `BatchProcessor`, `Dashboard`, and `ToolExecution`. I've established core services with `gemini.ts`. I'm prioritizing the high-level tools like Report Card Comment Writer and Essay Grader, and the batch workflow needs to be functional.


**Refining Batch Processing**

I'm currently addressing potential issues in the batch processing system. Processing for 30 students could be time-consuming and hit rate limits, so I'm building a sequential processor complete with a progress bar. The code also needs to handle large XML files and remain modular and concise. I'm focusing on ensuring responsiveness and correct Gemini SDK usage.

entries.description: Project metadata including permissions for the school AI tool station.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS support.
Global type definitions for the application.
Application constants and configuration.

import { Department, ToolMetadata, MenuItem } from './types';

export const DEPT_COLORS = {
  [Department.ADMIN]: {
    primary: 'bg-slate-700',
    text: 'text-slate-700',
    border: 'border-slate-700',
    light: 'bg-slate-50',
    hover: 'hover:bg-slate-100',
    accent: '#334155'
  },
  [Department.ACADEMIC]: {
    primary: 'bg-emerald-600',
    text: 'text-emerald-600',
    border: 'border-emerald-600',
    light: 'bg-emerald-50',
    hover: 'hover:bg-emerald-100',
    accent: '#059669'
  },
  [Department.STUDENT_AFFAIRS]: {
    primary: 'bg-orange-500',
    text: 'text-orange-500',
    border: 'border-orange-500',
    light: 'bg-orange-50',
    hover: 'hover:bg-orange-100',
    accent: '#f97316'
  },
  [Department.IT]: {
    primary: 'bg-indigo-600',
    text: 'text-indigo-600',
    border: 'border-indigo-600',
    light: 'bg-indigo-50',
    hover: 'hover:bg-indigo-100',
    accent: '#4f46e5'
  },
  [Department.DASHBOARD]: {
    primary: 'bg-blue-600',
    text: 'text-blue-600',
    border: 'border-blue-600',
    light: 'bg-blue-50',
    hover: 'hover:bg-blue-100',
    accent: '#2563eb'
  }
};

export const MENU_ITEMS: MenuItem[] = [
  { id: 'dashboard', label: '我的儀表板', icon: '📊', department: Department.DASHBOARD },
  {
    id: 'academic',
    label: '教務組',
    icon: '📚',
    children: [
      { id: 'academic-chinese', label: '中文科', icon: '✍️', department: Department.ACADEMIC },
      { id: 'academic-english', label: '英文科', icon: '🔤', department: Department.ACADEMIC },
      { id: 'academic-stem', label: '理科/電腦科', icon: '🔬', department: Department.ACADEMIC },
    ]
  },
  { id: 'affairs', label: '訓育組', icon: '👨‍🏫', department: Department.STUDENT_AFFAIRS },
  { id: 'admin', label: '行政組', icon: '🏢', department: Department.ADMIN },
  { id: 'it', label: '資訊組', icon: '💻', department: Department.IT },
];

export const TOOLS: ToolMetadata[] = [
  {
    id: 'comment-writer',
    name: '學生評語生成器',
    description: '根據學生成績、性格關鍵字及特殊表現，快速生成專業期末評語。',
    icon: '📝',
    department: Department.STUDENT_AFFAIRS,
    tags: ['批次處理', '訓育組'],
    isBatchSupported: true,
    inputs: [
      { key: 'name', label: '學生姓名', type: 'text', placeholder: '例如：王小明' },
      { key: 'score', label: '學業成績', type: 'number', placeholder: '0-100' },
      { key: 'traits', label: '性格特質', type: 'text', placeholder: '例如：活潑、認真、內向' },
      { key: 'special', label: '特殊事項', type: 'text', placeholder: '例如：班長、校隊成員' },
      { key: 'tone', label: '語氣選擇', type: 'select', options: ['正式', '親切', '激勵'] }
    ],
    promptTemplate: `請為學生 {{name}} 撰寫一份大約 150-200 字的專業期末評語。
學業成績為 {{score}} 分，性格特質為 {{traits}}，特殊表現包括 {{special}}。
請使用「{{tone}}」的語氣，包含正面鼓勵，並具體提出一項未來可改進的建議。`
  },
  {
    id: 'essay-grader',
    name: '作文批改助手',
    description: '自動分析學生作文，提供分項評分、優點分析及改進建議。',
    icon: '✍️',
    department: Department.ACADEMIC,
    tags: ['中文科', '批次處理'],
    isBatchSupported: true,
    inputs: [
      { key: 'essay', label: '作文全文', type: 'textarea', placeholder: '請粘貼學生的作文內容...' },
      { key: 'grade', label: '年級', type: 'select', options: ['小一', '小二', '小三', '小四', '小五', '小六', '中一', '中二', '中三'] },
      { key: 'criteria', label: '評分重點', type: 'text', placeholder: '例如：修辭、結構、創意' }
    ],
    promptTemplate: `請批改這篇 {{grade}} 年級學生的作文。
重點評分項目為：{{criteria}}。
作文內容：
{{essay}}

請輸出以下格式：
1. 分項評分 (100滿分)
2. 3-5項具體優點
3. 3-5項改進建議
4. 推薦佳句範例`
  },
  {
    id: 'tone-checker',
    name: '公文語氣檢查器',
    description: '確保對外公文及通知符合行政規範，修正常見口語錯誤。',
    icon: '📄',
    department: Department.ADMIN,
    tags: ['行政組', '正式'],
    isBatchSupported: false,
    inputs: [
      { key: 'content', label: '草擬內容', type: 'textarea', placeholder: '請輸入通知或公文內容...' },
      { key: 'audience', label: '目標對眾', type: 'select', options: ['全體家長', '全校教師', '全體學生', '校外單位'] }
    ],
    promptTemplate: `這是一份發送給 {{audience}} 的學校公文草稿。
請檢查並修正內容，使其符合正式行政規範。
草稿內容：
{{content}}

請提供：
1. 語氣專業度評分 (1-10)
2. 標記過於口語或不當的詞句
3. 修正後的完整版本`
  }
];

import React from 'react'; import { BarChart, Bar, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, ResponsiveContainer, AreaChart, Area } from 'recharts'; import { TOOLS } from '../constants'; const data = [ { name: '一月', usage: 120, tokens: 45000 }, { name: '二月', usage: 150, tokens: 52000 }, { name: '三月', usage: 220, tokens: 68000 }, { name: '四月', usage: 180, tokens: 59000 }, { name: '五月', usage: 260, tokens: 82000 }, { name: '六月', usage: 310, tokens: 95000 }, ]; const Dashboard: React.FC = () => { return (
{[ { label: '本月總使用次數', value: '310', delta: '+15%', color: 'blue' }, { label: '累計節省時間', value: '42 hrs', delta: '+12%', color: 'emerald' }, { label: 'API Token 消耗', value: '95k', delta: '+8%', color: 'amber' }, { label: '我的收藏工具', value: '8', delta: '穩定', color: 'indigo' }, ].map((stat, i) => (

{stat.label}

{stat.value}

{stat.delta}
))}

使用趨勢分析

最受歡迎工具

{TOOLS.slice(0, 3).map((tool, i) => (
{tool.icon}

{tool.name}

{tool.tags[0]}

{Math.floor(Math.random() * 50) + 20}次

熱門

))}
); }; export default Dashboard;

entries.description: Interactive tool execution interface.

metadata.message: Failed to run code assistant. Please try again.

payload.text: There was an unexpected error. Finish what you were doing.

**Revisiting the Design**

I'm back to the EduAI Hub design document! I was in the middle of implementation before the interruption, so I'm revisiting the details to refresh my memory and ensure everything aligns with the user's vision. I'm focusing on the core modules first.


**Finishing the Implementation**

I'm now fully immersed in the critical `index.tsx` file, which has stalled out a bit! The goal is to fully implement the application state management, linking up the sidebar, dashboard, and tool execution components. I'm focusing on the core logic and making sure the UI supports single tool execution initially, as requested. The components are ready, I just need to glue them together.


**Developing Core Routing**

I'm now zeroing in on `index.tsx`. The goal is to establish the core routing and state management to connect the Sidebar, Dashboard, and Tool Execution components. I need to make sure the UI transitions smoothly between the tool cards and the `ToolExecution` view upon tool selection. I'll prioritize implementing the essential logic for single tool execution first, then possibly integrate search, based on the design spec.

entries.description: Implement the main application entry point and state management for the EduAI Hub.
Fix the incomplete ToolExecution component from the previous turn.

payload.text:
**Adding New Tools**

I'm focusing on incorporating the QR Code Generator into the EduAI Hub. My current plan involves modifying the `constants.tsx` file to include this new tool definition, incorporating the HTML/JS logic. I am considering the implications on the existing IT tools, and striving to maintain modularity.


**Integrating Interactive Tools**

I'm now thinking about how to integrate the QR code generator and timer more seamlessly. Since the `ToolExecution` component is designed for AI prompts, I'm considering creating a special tool execution component for these interactive tools. I'm leaning towards defining these tools under `Department.IT` in `constants.tsx`, and making this special tool execution component. This should allow me to support the current "IT Tools" section, while preserving the interactive HTML/JS logic from the provided files.


**Refining Tool Integration**

I'm now reconsidering the approach for integrating the QR code and timer. My initial inclination was to create AI-driven versions, but the user's intent is clear. I need to modify the `ToolExecution` component to support direct execution of these functional tools. I will first update `constants.tsx` to include them in the IT section, then adjust the tool execution component. This aims to meet the prompt's requirements, and make sure that the IT tool section actually works.


**Defining Tool Types**

I'm now focusing on a more robust way to differentiate between tools. I'm going to update `types.ts` to include a `isUtility` flag within `ToolMetadata`. Then, I'll update `constants.tsx` with the new tools: "QR Code 生成器" and "倒計時器" within the `Department.IT` section. I'm thinking I'll incorporate a new system to update them as either "Formatting" or "Planning" help for now. Then, I will update the `ToolExecution.tsx` component to handle these new tool types.


**Extending The Hub**

I'm now shifting gears to make the hub extensible. My initial thought was to directly add the new tools to `constants.tsx`, which seems the most direct approach. However, after considering the user's intent to fulfill IT tools, and looking at the HTML for the QR code and timer, it seems their intent is to use standalone utilities, not AI-driven tools. I'm now considering how to update `ToolExecution.tsx` to handle these "self-contained utility" tools or provide structured help. I am now leaning toward adding them as AI-powered assistants.


**Defining New Tools**

I've decided to add two new tools directly in `constants.tsx` to integrate the timer and QR code functionalities. I'm focusing on their implementation and prompt design. I'm calling them "QR Code Content Generator" and "Lesson Timer Planner", with corresponding inputs and prompts tailored for Gemini integration. I plan to refine the inputs and prompt structure, making sure they integrate neatly into the "IT Tools" section.


**Refining Integration Plan**

I'm now focusing on integrating the new IT tools into `constants.tsx`, specifically the "智慧 QR Code 生成助手" and "課堂時間分配助手." I'm ensuring their tags and icons align correctly with the overall design. Then, I plan on enhancing the `ToolExecution.tsx` UI to align with a "senior engineer" feel, which includes better layouts for results. I am now rethinking this, and seeing if there are any other department tools mentioned in the design.


**Adding Standalone Utilities**

I'm now integrating the QR code and timer as standalone utility tools. I've analyzed the provided HTML and realized they rely on external libraries (QRCode.js for the QR code and standard JS for the timer). I am planning to integrate `renderSpecialUtility` function into `ToolExecution`. The goal is to provide a way to directly execute these tools. I will add this to the `constants.tsx` to handle the required components. I believe this will be a good user experience for the IT tools section.

entries.description: Add new IT department tools (QR Code Assistant and Lesson Timer Planner) to the TOOLS constant.

  },

    icon: '📄',

    tags: ['行政組', '正式'],
    isBatchSupported: false,
    inputs: [
      { key: 'content', label: '草擬內容', type: 'textarea', placeholder: '請輸入通知或公文內容...' },
      { key: 'audience', label: '目標對眾', type: 'select', options: ['全體家長', '全校教師', '全體學生', '校外單位'] }
    ],
    promptTemplate: `這是一份發送給 {{audience}} 的學校公文草稿。
請檢查並修正內容，使其符合正式行政規範。
草稿內容：
{{content}}

請提供：
1. 語氣專業度評分 (1-10)
2. 標記過於口語或不當的詞句
3. 修正後的完整版本`
  },
  {
    id: 'qr-assistant',
    name: '智慧 QR Code 內容助手',
    description: '協助校方生成並優化 QR Code 連結內容，提供安全性檢查與短連結建議。',
    icon: '🔗',
    department: Department.IT,
    tags: ['資訊組', '安全'],
    isBatchSupported: false,
    inputs: [
      { key: 'url', label: '目標網址/內容', type: 'text', placeholder: 'https://school.edu.hk/activity' },
      { key: 'purpose', label: '使用場景', type: 'select', options: ['海報宣傳', '聯絡簿', '校園展示屏', '學生手冊'] }

此 QR Code 將用於「{{purpose}}」。
請提供：
1. 連結安全性檢查建議。
2. 針對該場景的標題與引導文案建議。
3. 若連結過長，請建議是否需要使用校內短連結服務。
4. 提供一個結構化的 JSON 格式，包含 title, description, 和 suggested_cta。`
  },
  {
    id: 'lesson-timer-planner',
    name: '智慧課堂時間分配器',
    description: '智慧分配課堂時間，為老師提供精準的各環節計時建議與進度提示。',
    icon: '⏲️',

    tags: ['資訊組', '教學工具'],
    isBatchSupported: false,

      { key: 'topic', label: '教學主題', type: 'text', placeholder: '例如：光學反射定律' },
      { key: 'duration', label: '總時長 (分鐘)', type: 'number', placeholder: '35, 40 或 80' },
      { key: 'complexity', label: '課程難度', type: 'select', options: ['引導式', '密集練習型', '實驗/動手做', '總結測驗型'] }
    ],
    promptTemplate: `請為教學主題「{{topic}}」規劃一個 {{duration}} 分鐘的課堂時間分配表。
課程類型為「{{complexity}}」。
請輸出：
1. 詳細的時間線 (例如：0-5min 導入...)。
2. 每個環節的「關鍵提示詞」給老師參考。
3. 針對最後 5 分鐘的總結建議。
4. 格式請使用表格呈現，並包含每個階段的目標。`
  }
];

payload.text: no. JUST EMBED THIS TOOLS TO THE 資訊組

**Considering Tool Integration**

I'm currently focused on how to best integrate the "QR Code Assistant" and "Lesson Timer Planner" into the "EduAI Hub" platform for the IT Department. I've taken note of the provided source code and am now thinking about the optimal architecture for seamless integration and user experience within the existing framework. My primary concern is maintainability and scalability, given the platform's long-term goals.


**Refining Integration Approach**

I've realized the user wants the core functionality of the QR code generator and timer, not just descriptions. I'm now planning how to best incorporate the provided HTML/JS code into the React-based "EduAI Hub." This involves creating specific components or modifying `ToolExecution` to render the interactive UIs. I'm reconciling this with the previous focus on AI-powered content generation for these tools, as suggested by their smart names. I'm focusing on the best user experience that can balance both the AI and code functionality.


**Clarifying Standalone Tool Embeds**

I've realized the user wants standalone tool embeds, and I was focusing too much on AI-driven features. My plan is now to directly integrate the provided HTML/JS into the IT department section. I'll update `constants.tsx` (confirmed) and modify `ToolExecution.tsx` or create a `CustomToolRenderer` to render the interactive HTML/JS for the QR code and lesson timer tools. This aligns better with the user's intent to "JUST EMBED THESE TOOLS".


**Implementing React Components**

I'm now focusing on implementing the `QRCodeTool.tsx` and `TimerTool.tsx` components. I plan to port the functionality from the given HTML/JS code into these React components. In the case of the QR code generator, I'll leverage `qrcode.react` from esm.sh for efficient integration, having decided against directly importing external scripts. For the timer, I will port all the logic from the given script file to React state. I'll also modify `ToolExecution.tsx` to handle the conditional rendering of the custom UIs based on the tool ID, ensuring a smooth user experience. Finally, I'll review and update `constants.tsx` to align the tool definitions with these new components.

entries.description: Update tool definitions for IT department to include QR Code and Timer tools with specific IDs for custom rendering.

import { Department, ToolMetadata, MenuItem } from './types';

export const DEPT_COLORS = {
  [Department.ADMIN]: {
    primary: 'bg-slate-700',
    text: 'text-slate-700',
    border: 'border-slate-700',
    light: 'bg-slate-50',
    hover: 'hover:bg-slate-100',
    accent: '#334155'
  },
  [Department.ACADEMIC]: {
    primary: 'bg-emerald-600',
    text: 'text-emerald-600',
    border: 'border-emerald-600',
    light: 'bg-emerald-50',
    hover: 'hover:bg-emerald-100',
    accent: '#059669'
  },
  [Department.STUDENT_AFFAIRS]: {
    primary: 'bg-orange-500',
    text: 'text-orange-500',
    border: 'border-orange-500',
    light: 'bg-orange-50',
    hover: 'hover:bg-orange-100',
    accent: '#f97316'
  },
  [Department.IT]: {
    primary: 'bg-indigo-600',
    text: 'text-indigo-600',
    border: 'border-indigo-600',
    light: 'bg-indigo-50',
    hover: 'hover:bg-indigo-100',
    accent: '#4f46e5'
  },
  [Department.DASHBOARD]: {
    primary: 'bg-blue-600',
    text: 'text-blue-600',
    border: 'border-blue-600',
    light: 'bg-blue-50',
    hover: 'hover:bg-blue-100',
    accent: '#2563eb'
  }
};

export const MENU_ITEMS: MenuItem[] = [
  { id: 'dashboard', label: '我的儀表板', icon: '📊', department: Department.DASHBOARD },
  {
    id: 'academic',
    label: '教務組',
    icon: '📚',
    children: [
      { id: 'academic-chinese', label: '中文科', icon: '✍️', department: Department.ACADEMIC },
      { id: 'academic-english', label: '英文科', icon: '🔤', department: Department.ACADEMIC },
      { id: 'academic-stem', label: '理科/電腦科', icon: '🔬', department: Department.ACADEMIC },
    ]
  },
  { id: 'affairs', label: '訓育組', icon: '👨‍🏫', department: Department.STUDENT_AFFAIRS },
  { id: 'admin', label: '行政組', icon: '🏢', department: Department.ADMIN },
  { id: 'it', label: '資訊組', icon: '💻', department: Department.IT },
];

export const TOOLS: ToolMetadata[] = [
  {
    id: 'comment-writer',
    name: '學生評語生成器',
    description: '根據學生成績、性格關鍵字及特殊表現，快速生成專業期末評語。',
    icon: '📝',
    department: Department.STUDENT_AFFAIRS,
    tags: ['批次處理', '訓育組'],
    isBatchSupported: true,
    inputs: [
      { key: 'name', label: '學生姓名', type: 'text', placeholder: '例如：王小明' },
      { key: 'score', label: '學業成績', type: 'number', placeholder: '0-100' },
      { key: 'traits', label: '性格特質', type: 'text', placeholder: '例如：活潑、認真、內向' },
      { key: 'special', label: '特殊事項', type: 'text', placeholder: '例如：班長、校隊成員' },
      { key: 'tone', label: '語氣選擇', type: 'select', options: ['正式', '親切', '激勵'] }
    ],
    promptTemplate: `請為學生 {{name}} 撰寫一份大約 150-200 字的專業期末評語。
學業成績為 {{score}} 分，性格特質為 {{traits}}，特殊表現包括 {{special}}。
請使用「{{tone}}」的語氣，包含正面鼓勵，並具體提出一項未來可改進的建議。`
  },
  {
    id: 'essay-grader',
    name: '作文批改助手',
    description: '自動分析學生作文，提供分項評分、優點分析及改進建議。',
    icon: '✍️',
    department: Department.ACADEMIC,
    tags: ['中文科', '批次處理'],
    isBatchSupported: true,
    inputs: [
      { key: 'essay', label: '作文全文', type: 'textarea', placeholder: '請粘貼學生的作文內容...' },
      { key: 'grade', label: '年級', type: 'select', options: ['小一', '小二', '小三', '小四', '小五', '小六', '中一', '中二', '中三'] },
      { key: 'criteria', label: '評分重點', type: 'text', placeholder: '例如：修辭、結構、創意' }
    ],
    promptTemplate: `請批改這篇 {{grade}} 年級學生的作文。
重點評分項目為：{{criteria}}。
作文內容：
{{essay}}

請輸出以下格式：
1. 分項評分 (100滿分)
2. 3-5項具體優點
3. 3-5項改進建議
4. 推薦佳句範例`
  },
  {
    id: 'tone-checker',
    name: '公文語氣檢查器',
    description: '確保對外公文及通知符合行政規範，修正常見口語錯誤。',
    icon: '📄',
    department: Department.ADMIN,
    tags: ['行政組', '正式'],
    isBatchSupported: false,
    inputs: [
      { key: 'content', label: '草擬內容', type: 'textarea', placeholder: '請輸入通知或公文內容...' },
      { key: 'audience', label: '目標對眾', type: 'select', options: ['全體家長', '全校教師', '全體學生', '校外單位'] }
    ],
    promptTemplate: `這是一份發送給 {{audience}} 的學校公文草稿。
請檢查並修正內容，使其符合正式行政規範。
草稿內容：
{{content}}

請提供：
1. 語氣專業度評分 (1-10)
2. 標記過於口語或不當的詞句
3. 修正後的完整版本`
  },
  {
    id: 'qr-generator-tool',
    name: 'QR Code 生成器',
    description: '快速將網址或文字轉換為 QR Code，支援下載功能。',
    icon: '📱',
    department: Department.IT,
    tags: ['資訊組', '實用工具'],
    isBatchSupported: false,
    inputs: [], // Custom tool
    promptTemplate: ''
  },
  {
    id: 'class-timer-tool',
    name: '計時器',
    description: '專業教學用計時器，支援全螢幕顯示與補時功能記錄。',
    icon: '⏱️',
    department: Department.IT,
    tags: ['資訊組', '教學工具'],
    isBatchSupported: false,
    inputs: [], // Custom tool
    promptTemplate: ''
  }
];

entries.description: Update ToolExecution to support custom rendering for specific IT tools (QR Code and Timer).
Implementation of the custom QR Code Tool ported from provided HTML/JS logic.
Implementation of the custom Timer Tool ported from provided HTML/JS logic, including fullscreen and added-time tracking.

payload.text: Can you make a admin page for edit and use GOOGLE SSO to login to verify the identity

**Considering Admin Page Needs**

I'm currently focused on the Admin Page for "EduAI Hub". The core requirements seem to be an "Edit Functionality," which I interpret as likely CRUD operations on the tools and app settings. I also see the need for Google SSO to be included.


**Implementing Admin Authentication**

I'm now zeroing in on admin authentication. My plan involves a simulated Google SSO using GIS for the UI and integration, given the absence of a real backend. The logic will toggle admin state based on login success. I'll need a login screen for unauthenticated users, then an admin dashboard/editor for authorized users, with edit features to manage the static `TOOLS` constant via simulated local state changes.


**Adding Admin Functionality**

I'm now integrating the admin panel into the application. I've added an admin link to the sidebar, which conditionally displays based on authentication. I'm updating `index.html` to include the Google Identity Services script. New types for `User` are added in `types.ts`, and I've added a new "Admin" entry to the `MENU_ITEMS` constant. The new `AdminPanel.tsx` component will handle the Google SSO login button and display the admin interface. I'm globally managing authentication state in `index.tsx` and handling navigation. I'm focusing on the Google SSO implementation with the standard `div` approach and a school email check for permissions.

To implement an Admin Page with Google SSO authentication, we need to update the core structure to handle authentication states and add a dedicated administrative interface.

### Specification:
1.  **Authentication**:
    *   Integrated **Google Identity Services (GSI)**.
    *   A "Login with Google" portal that appears when accessing the Admin section.
    *   Identity verification logic (simulated for this environment but using real GIS code structures).
2.  **Admin Panel**:
    *   **Tool Management**: Ability to view and "edit" the configuration of existing tools (Name, Description, Tags).
    *   **Usage Logs**: A detailed table showing all AI interactions across the school.
    *   **System Status**: Oversight of API usage and token consumption.
3.  **Visual Appearance**:
    *   The Admin panel will use a "Command Center" aesthetic—darker, data-heavy, with high-contrast UI elements to distinguish it from the teacher's tool-focused views.
    *   Smooth transitions between the public dashboard and the gated admin area.

entries.description: Add Google Identity Services script for SSO support.
Add AdminUser and update Department to include Admin Management.
Add System Admin menu item and color scheme.

entries.description: Add specific styling for the System Admin menu item.

payload.text: normal users can use google SSO to login

**Expanding Google SSO Access**

I'm currently working on extending Google SSO to all users, not just admins. This involves integrating the Google login process across the entire application and ensuring proper session management for regular users and admins alike. The UI will also require updates to accommodate this expanded functionality.


**Refining App Logic**

Okay, I've outlined the logic flow now. On app load, I'll check for a logged-in user. If none, they hit a new login screen, prompting Google SSO. If logged in, the user data populates the sidebar and the app loads. I'll simplify the AdminPanel now. The next steps will involve updating `index.tsx` for global login and type updates.

entries.description: Enable global Google SSO for all users and implement a unified login screen.
Update Sidebar to receive and display real user information from SSO.
Remove the internal login logic from AdminPanel as authentication is now global.

diffs.replacement:
import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react'; import { TOOLS } from '../constants'; import { AdminUser, UsageLog } from '../types'; import { BarChart, Bar, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts'; interface AdminPanelProps { user: AdminUser | null; onLogout: () => void; } const AdminPanel: React.FC = ({ user, onLogout }) => { const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<'tools' | 'logs' | 'settings'>('tools'); const [editingToolId, setEditingToolId] = useState(null); // Mock usage logs const logs: UsageLog[] = [ { id: '1', timestamp: '2023-10-25 14:30', toolId: 'comment-writer', toolName: '學生評語生成器', userEmail: 'chen.dw@school.edu.hk', inputData: '王小明, 85分', status: 'success' }, { id: '2', timestamp: '2023-10-25 15:10', toolId: 'essay-grader', toolName: '作文批改助手', userEmail: 'lee.sm@school.edu.hk', inputData: '中三作文', status: 'success' }, { id: '3', timestamp: '2023-10-25 15:45', toolId: 'tone-checker', toolName: '公文語氣檢查器', userEmail: 'admin@school.edu.hk', inputData: '校慶通知', status: 'success' }, ]; // Since login is now global, we just check role here if (!user || user.role !== 'superadmin') { return (
